judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high revenue expansion but currently unprofitable and trading at a high multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 44.8%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — significant future growth projected.
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may justify the current valuation.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — healthy short-term liquidity.
Total cash $439.70M vs Total debt $280.42M — manageable debt load.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is currently losing money.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — negative returns on capital.
Forward P/E 36.73x and Price/Sales 27.74x — valuation is high, especially given unprofitability.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Beta 3.76 — stock is highly volatile relative to the market.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) is seeing some selling pressure alongside other AI leaders, despite Mark Cuban advocating for a focus on foundational AI. The stock's performance appears to be influenced by broader market sentiment affecting tech and AI-related equities, with no specific company news driving the recent downturn. Investors should monitor the upcoming earnings report for any signs of operational strength or weakness.

headlines.title: Dow Jones Futures: Trump Iran Delay Saves Dow, But Sandisk, Bloom Energy, AI Leaders Sell Off
Applied Optoelectronics Inc. (AAOI): Mark Cuban Urges Focus on Foundational AI
